 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2","title":"BOCPD Resize Coalescer Upgrade","description":"Background
- Resize coalescer currently uses a heuristic burst/steady regime with hysteresis thresholds.
- We want a mathematically principled, adaptive regime detector with formal properties.

Goal
- Replace heuristic regime detection with Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection (BOCPD) to classify steady vs burst behavior.
- Preserve existing invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, deterministic decisions.

Key Concepts
- Run-length posterior over regime changes, hazard function for change probability.
- Decision uses posterior mass over burst regime to choose coalescing delay.
- Computation kept O(K) with run-length truncation.

Success Criteria
- Coalescer remains deterministic and respects hard deadlines.
- Fewer wasted renders during resize storms with no additional latency in steady mode.
- Decision logs include posterior summary for auditability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T23:00:53.918070141Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T23:00:53.918050614Z","close_reason":"BOCPD integration complete. Core functionality: (1) BocpdDetector in bocpd.rs with exponential hazard + run-length truncation at K=100. (2) Integrated into ResizeCoalescer via enable_bocpd config. (3) update_regime() uses BOCPD with heuristic fallback. (4) Accessor methods: bocpd_p_burst(), bocpd_recommended_delay(). (5) 13 BOCPD tests + 4 property tests pass. Remaining P3 subtasks (2.4 logging, 2.6 simulation) are optional enhancements.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","title":"BOCPD design: hazard, run-length cap, decision rule","description":"Background
- We need a formal regime detector for resize bursts that replaces heuristic thresholds.

Task
- Define BOCPD model: observation = inter-arrival time or event rate; hazard function H(r);
  predictive likelihood for steady vs burst regimes; run-length truncation K for O(K) update.
- Specify decision rule: use posterior mass on burst regime to select coalescing delay.

Details
- Choose observation model (e.g., exponential for inter-arrival, or Poisson for counts).
- Hazard: constant hazard or adaptive based on recent rate.
- Provide defaults tuned for responsiveness on large terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Design doc in issue description is implementable as-is.
- Explicit complexity bounds and deterministic update rules.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:33:05.533775751Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:33:05.533751866Z","close_reason":"Implemented BOCPD design with hazard function, run-length cap (K=100), exponential observation model for inter-arrival times, and decision rule using P(burst) thresholds.  {"id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","title":"Spec: dirty-span model, invariants, and merge policy","description":"Background: dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates. Task: specify per-row span data model, merge/union policy, max span cap, overflow fallback to full-row scan, deterministic ordering, and reset semantics via clear_dirty. Considerations: scissor/opacity interactions, optional guard bands, config defaults, and evidence log fields. Output: written spec captured in notes.","notes":"Dirty-Span Model (Spec v1)

Motivation
- Dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates: scanning full rows wastes work.
- Dirty-span tracking records per-row modified x-ranges to reduce diff scan cost while preserving soundness.

Data Model
- For each row y, maintain a small ordered list of spans: Span { x0: u16, x1: u16 } half-open.
- Spans are non-overlapping, sorted by x0 ascending.
- Per-row full_row/overflow flag indicates the row should be scanned fully; when true, spans list is empty.
- Optional guard band (merge_gap) merges spans that are within ≤ merge_gap cells.

Invariants (Must Hold)
1) Soundness: for any cell (x,y) mutated since last clear, ∃ span in row y such that x ∈ [x0,x1).
2) Sorted + non-overlapping spans per row (after merge).
3) Deterministic merge order: given the same mutation sequence, span lists are identical.
4) Full-row flag dominates: if full_row[y] = true, spans[y] is empty and row is scanned fully.

Merge / Union Policy
- Insert span (x0,x1) into row y:
  - If full_row[y] => no-op (already full).
  - Insert at position by x0 (binary search recommended).
  - Merge with neighbors when overlaps or when gap ≤ merge_gap.
  - After merge, if span_count > max_spans_per_row => set full_row[y]=true and clear spans.
- Default implementation values (current): merge_gap = 1, max_spans_per_row = 64.
- Optional future: make these configurable; add coverage threshold to flip to full_row.

Mutation API (Conceptual)
- mark_dirty_span(y, x0, x1)
- mark_dirty_cell(y, x) => mark_dirty_span(y, x, x+1)
- mark_dirty_rect(rect): for each row in rect, call mark_dirty_span(y, rect.x, rect.right())

Reset Semantics
- clear_dirty_spans(): clears all spans and full_row flags.
- On Buffer::new, initialize all rows as full_row to guarantee first diff is full.

Interaction with Existing Dirty Rows
- Dirty-span is a strict refinement; dirty_rows is a coarse fallback.
- If both exist, diff strategy may choose:
  - full_row[y] => full scan (row-skip still allowed)
  - spans[y].is_empty() => skip row
  - spans[y] => scan only spans
- False positives are allowed; false negatives are forbidden.

Scissor / Opacity / Compositing
- Only record spans for actual writes that pass scissor checks.
- Opacity compositing may produce no net change, but still mark span; false positives are OK.
- When scissor stacks are used, mark spans only for the intersected row segments.

Determinism & Ordering
- Use Vec/SmallVec per row to avoid hash-based ordering.
- Merge order is deterministic: left-to-right; stable tie-breakers for equal x0.

Evidence / Telemetry Fields (proposed)
- dirty_span_rows, dirty_span_total, dirty_span_overflows
- max_spans_per_row, merge_gap, span_coverage_cells
- rows_full_dirty

Failure Modes + Fallbacks
- Excessive fragmentation => full_row fallback
- Span list overflow => full_row fallback
- Resize => re-init to full dirty

Tests (Spec-level)
- Unit: merge behavior (overlap, adjacency, guard band, overflow)
- Property: soundness vs random mutation sets (spans cover all mutated cells)
- Regression: deterministic output for identical mutation sequences
- E2E: span stats logged in JSONL under fixed seed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:30:27.225108113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:16:10.481846217Z","close_reason":"Completed dirty-span spec in docs/spec/state-machines.md section 3.14","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.7.1","title":"Spec: tile size + summed-area table design","description":"Background: SAT path needs a precise design. Task: define tile size, bitmap encoding, prefix-sum formula, memory layout, dense fallback thresholds, deterministic tile scan order, config defaults, and evidence log fields. Output: written spec in notes.","notes":"Spec: Tile size + summed-area table (SAT) design

Goal
- Use a dirty bitmap + SAT to skip empty tiles while preserving exact diff output order.

Tile size (defaults)
- tile_w = 16, tile_h = 8 (128 cells per tile).
- Power-of-two preferred for fast div/mod; clamp to [8, 64].
- tiles_x = ceil(width / tile_w), tiles_y = ceil(height / tile_h).
- Only enable tile path when total_cells >= min_cells_for_tiles (default 12000 ~= 200x60).

Dirty bitmap encoding
- Add dirty_bits: Vec<u8> length = width * height.
- Encoding: 0 = clean, 1 = dirty (superset of changed cells).
- On any cell mutation (set/set_raw/get_mut/fill/clear_with/etc), set dirty_bits[idx]=1.
- Track dirty_cells count; increment only on 0->1 transitions.
- Bulk mutation APIs that expose full buffer (cells_mut) or clear the buffer set dirty_all=true and dirty_cells=total_cells (skip per-cell loops).
- clear_dirty() resets dirty_rows, dirty_bits (memset to 0), dirty_cells=0, dirty_all=false.

SAT build + tile mask
- Build per-tile counts by scanning dirty_bits once per frame:
  - tile_index = (y / tile_h) * tiles_x + (x / tile_w)
  - tile_counts[tile_index] += dirty_bits[idx]
- Build SAT over tile grid using 1-based indexing:
  - sat[(ty+1, tx+1)] = tile_counts[ty,tx]
    + sat[(ty, tx+1)] + sat[(ty+1, tx)] - sat[(ty, tx)]
- Use u32 for sat/tile_counts (max sum <= total_cells).
- Derive dirty_tiles mask: dirty_tiles[t] = tile_counts[t] > 0 (or via SAT query for any sub-rect).
- Compute dirty_tile_count, dirty_tile_ratio.

Deterministic scan order (must match row-major)
- Preserve exact ordering of BufferDiff::compute by scanning rows in order:
  for y in 0..height:
    ty = y / tile_h
    for tx in 0..tiles_x:
      if dirty_tiles[ty, tx] { scan x in [tx*tile_w, min(width, (tx+1)*tile_w)) }
- Within each row segment, reuse existing scan_row_changes_range to keep row-major ordering.
- This guarantees identical change list ordering vs full scan.

Dense fallback thresholds
- Skip tile path and fall back to DirtyRows/Full if any is true:
  - dirty_all == true
  - dirty_cells / total_cells >= dense_cell_ratio (default 0.25)
  - dirty_tiles / total_tiles >= dense_tile_ratio (default 0.60)
  - tiles_x * tiles_y > max_tiles (default 4096)
  - SAT build overflow (should not happen with u32; still guard).

Config defaults (new TileDiffConfig)
- enabled: true
- tile_w: 16, tile_h: 8
- min_cells_for_tiles: 12000
- dense_cell_ratio: 0.25
- dense_tile_ratio: 0.60
- max_tiles: 4096
- deterministic_tie_break: row-major scan order (no sorting)

Evidence log fields (JSONL)
- event: "diff_tile_skip"
- frame, width, height
- tile_w, tile_h, tiles_x, tiles_y
- dirty_cells, dirty_tiles, total_tiles
- dirty_cell_ratio, dirty_tile_ratio
- skipped_tiles, scanned_tiles
- fallback_reason: {disabled, small_screen, dirty_all, dense_cells, dense_tiles, too_many_tiles, overflow}
- scan_cost_estimate: {cells_scanned_est, tiles_scanned, sat_build_cells}

Notes
- SAT build is O(total_cells) over 1-byte bitmap (much cheaper than 16B cell scans).
- False positives in dirty_bits are safe; they only reduce skipping, not correctness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:53:17.824810394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:18:51.965648939Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:18:51.965627960Z","close_reason":"Spec completed in notes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:53:17.824810394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Task: define widget value and cost signals with safe defaults and deterministic sources; include priority, staleness, focus, size, and recent cost; document normalization. Acceptance: signals available to runtime.","notes":"Spec draft — Widget value/cost signals (deterministic + normalized)

Goal
Define a stable signal schema + defaults for greedy refresh selection. Signals must be deterministic, cheap to compute, and available to the runtime after `Model::view()`.

Signal schema (per widget)
- widget_id: stable u64 (prefer HitId or explicit widget registry id).
- essential: bool (Widget::is_essential).
- priority: f32 in [0,1].
- staleness_ms: u64 (now - last_render_ms).
- focus_boost: f32 in [0,1] (1 when focused, 0 otherwise).
- interaction_boost: f32 in [0,1] (recent input/hover/click).
- area_cells: u32 (width * height).
- cost_estimate_us: f32 (fallback estimate if no measurement).
- recent_cost_us: f32 (EMA of measured render time when available).
- estimate_source: enum {Measured, AreaFallback, FixedDefault}.

Deterministic sources
- widget_id: stable ordering from registry; if none, hash of (widget name + area rect) is allowed but must be deterministic.
- essential: from widget trait.
- staleness_ms: runtime clock (monotonic) minus last_render timestamp stored in runtime map keyed by widget_id.
- focus_boost: from focus manager or current active HitId; if not available, 0.
- interaction_boost: from input events registered against widget_id; if not available, 0.
- area_cells: from render area rect (width * height).
- cost_estimate_us: area_cells * cost_per_cell_us (defaults below), clamped.
- recent_cost_us: EMA of measured render time (if widget instrumentation exists), otherwise cost_estimate_us.

Normalization (for greedy selection)
- priority_norm = clamp(priority, 0, 1).
- staleness_norm = min(staleness_ms / max_stale_ms, 1.0).
- focus_norm = clamp(focus_boost, 0, 1).
- interaction_norm = clamp(interaction_boost, 0, 1).
- area_norm = sqrt(area_cells) / sqrt(area_cap_cells) (cap at 1.0).
- cost_norm = recent_cost_us / budget_us (cap at 1.0, floor at min_cost_us / budget_us).

Defaults
- priority = 0.5 (neutral).
- focus_boost = 0.0, interaction_boost = 0.0.
- max_stale_ms = 2000.
- area_cap_cells = 10_000.
- cost_per_cell_us = 0.02, min_cost_us = 5, max_cost_us = 5000.
- EMA alpha for recent_cost_us = 0.2 (new = alpha * measured + (1-alpha) * old).

Availability to runtime (non-API-breaking plan)
- Add `WidgetSignals` struct in ftui-render (near Frame) and a `Frame::register_widget_signal(signal)` method.
- `Frame` stores `widget_signals: Vec<WidgetSignals>`; `Program::render_buffer` can read/drain this vector after `view()`.
- No dependency on ftui-widgets; widgets or app code may optionally register signals during render.
- If no signals registered, runtime uses empty list and falls back to essential-only or full render.

Notes
- This spec is compatible with the bd-3e1t.9.1 utility function (priority/staleness/focus/area/cost inputs).
- Determinism: all defaults and normalizations are pure functions of stable inputs; no randomness.

Implementation status
- Added `WidgetSignal` + `CostEstimateSource` in `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs`.
- Added `Frame::register_widget_signal`, `Frame::take_widget_signals`.
- Program captures signals after `view()` and exposes `last_widget_signals()`.
- Updated `crates/ftui-widgets/src/help.rs` Frame literals with new field.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:54:28.761904443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:35:08.993283604Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:35:08.993260841Z","close_reason":"Signals defined + plumbing added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:54:28.761904443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:54:43.860452164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-jbg","title":"Implement forms and picker widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: FORMS AND PICKERS

## Background
Interactive forms and picker dialogs are common UI patterns for configuration, data entry, and selection. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Form Widget
- Field types: TextInput, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Number
- Validation support with error messages
- Tab navigation between fields
- Submit/Cancel actions
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Required field indicators

### Form API

pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<FormField>,
    focused: usize,
    on_submit: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&FormData)>>,
}

pub enum FormField {
    Text { label: String, value: String, placeholder: Option<String> },
    Checkbox { label: String, checked: bool },
    Radio { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Select { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Number { label: String, value: i64, min: Option<i64>, max: Option<i64> },
}

impl Form {
    pub fn new(fields: Vec<FormField>) -> Self;
    pub fn focus_next(&mut self);
    pub fn focus_prev(&mut self);
    pub fn submit(&self) -> FormData;
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<ValidationError>;
}

### Picker Widgets
- FilePicker: Browse filesystem with filtering
- ColorPicker: Visual color selection
- DatePicker: Calendar-based date selection
- ConfirmDialog: Yes/No confirmation

### Picker API

pub struct FilePicker {
    current_dir: PathBuf,
    filter: Option<String>,  // glob pattern
    show_hidden: bool,
    selection: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl FilePicker {
    pub fn new(start_dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Self;
    pub fn filter(mut self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn show_hidden(mut self, show: bool) -> Self;
    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&Path>;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Form field navigation works
- [ ] Validation errors display correctly
- [ ] Submit collects all values
- [ ] FilePicker navigates directories
- [ ] ColorPicker returns valid RGB

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All form field types implemented
- [ ] Keyboard navigation complete
- [ ] FilePicker with glob filtering
- [ ] ConfirmDialog with Yes/No
- [ ] Accessible focus indicators

 {"id":"bd-k4lj","title":"Terminal Capability Simulator","description":"## Overview
Provide testing infrastructure that simulates various terminal capabilities for comprehensive compatibility testing.

## Rationale
- Real terminal testing requires many different terminals
- CI environments cannot run every terminal emulator
- Capability degradation paths need verification
- Helps developers test edge cases without hardware

## Core Components
1. **Virtual Terminal**: In-memory terminal that tracks state
2. **Capability Profiles**: Predefined profiles (xterm-256, vt100, dumb, etc.)
3. **Capability Override**: Runtime capability injection for testing
4. **Behavior Simulation**: Simulate quirks of specific terminals
5. **Recording/Playback**: Record real terminal sessions for replay

## Implementation Strategy
- Extend ftui-pty with VirtualTerminal that implements terminal traits
- Create capability profiles as static configurations
- Add CapabilityOverride to ftui-core for testing
- Integrate with snapshot testing in ftui-harness

## Predefined Profiles
- xterm-256color: Full features
- xterm: 16 colors only
- vt100: No colors, basic cursor
- dumb: Minimal capabilities
- screen: tmux/screen quirks
- windows-conhost: Windows Console Host behavior

## Testing Requirements
- Meta-tests: verify simulator matches real terminals
- Degradation tests: app behavior with reduced capabilities
- Cross-profile snapshot comparisons

## Success Criteria
- Can run full test suite without real terminal
- Capability detection code is thoroughly tested
- CI catches capability-related regressions

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
